text: <par>The stopped tone in the second syllable is unexplained. Note, however, the final <latinform>-q</latinform> in some Qiang forms. Akha <latinform>a˯loe˯</latinform> has been tentatively assigned to <xref ref="1154">#1154</xref>, below.</par>
<par>The stopped tone in the second syllable is unexplained. Note, however, the final <latinform>-q</latinform> in some Qiang forms, such as Qiang (Mawo) <latinform>liq</latinform> 'penis'.</par>
<par>Second syllable probably means 'friend' - the placenta is the foetus' pal.</par>
<par>The final <latinform>-ah</latinform> in Lewis's transcription stands for the nasal rhyme <latinform>aŋ</latinform>.</par>
